name: Mahalakshmi
description: Monthly financial assistance of ₹2,500 to every woman, LPG cylinders at ₹500, and free travel for women in TSRTC buses
promised: ₹2,500/month to every woman head of household, LPG at ₹500, free bus travel for women
delivered: Free bus travel for women implemented (serving ~35 lakh women daily, ₹7,422 crore reimbursed to TSRTC). ₹500 gas cylinder subsidy launched Feb 27, 2024 for ~40 lakh white ration card families. ₹2,500/month cash transfer: zero disbursements as of March 2026 (818+ days). Application forms collected in Jan 2024 but no implementation steps initiated. The cash transfer was the centrepiece of this guarantee.

name: Rythu Bharosa
description: ₹15,000/acre financial assistance to farmers, crop loan waiver up to ₹2 lakh, interest-free loans up to ₹3 lakh
promised: ₹15,000/acre/year to farmers and tenant farmers, ₹12,000/year to agri labour, crop loan waiver up to ₹2L, interest-free loans up to ₹3L
delivered: Rythu Bharosa launched Jan 26, 2025 at ₹12,000/acre/year (20% below promised ₹15,000). Day-one: ₹569 crore to 4,41,911 farmers. Government claims ₹9,000 crore reached 70 lakh farmers in 9 days. Crop loan waiver: ~₹20,617 crore disbursed to ~25 lakh farmers; target was 50 lakh (₹31,000 crore). ₹12,000 to agri labour: not started. 793 farmer suicides recorded Dec 2023-Nov 2025 (vs 178 in BRS's final year 2022).

name: Gruha Jyothi
description: Free electricity up to 200 units per household per month
promised: 200 units of free electricity to every household
delivered: Operational since February 2024. ~42.25 lakh households receiving zero bills. Issues: ~2.50 lakh eligible applicants in Hyderabad still receiving bills. Billing cycle inconsistencies push some households above the 200-unit threshold. Reports of declining beneficiary numbers suggest possible silent rollback.

name: Indiramma Indlu
description: House site for families without own house and ₹5 lakh financial assistance for construction
promised: Free house site + ₹5 lakh construction assistance to every homeless family
delivered: 4.5 lakh houses sanctioned with ₹5 lakh assistance each. First houses inaugurated on September 3, 2025 — 21 months after taking office. Full-scale construction has not begun in most districts. Budget allocation: ₹5,800 crore; ₹800 crore spent (14% utilization).

name: Yuva Vikasam
description: Fill 2 lakh government jobs in the first year, Vidya Bharosa cards for students, skill development
promised: 2,00,000 government jobs in first year, Vidya Bharosa education cards (₹5 lakh/student), free internet for students, ₹4,000/month unemployment allowance
delivered: ~57,946 posts filled by March 2025 (29% of target). Key: Group 1 (562), Group 4 (8,084), Mega DSC (10,006 teachers). SC sub-categorization froze new recruitment Oct 2024-Apr 2025. Vidya Bharosa cards: zero implementation. ₹4,000 unemployment allowance: zero implementation — Deputy CM publicly denied in Dec 2024 that Congress had made this promise. Youth unemployment at 20.1% (vs 14.6% national average).

name: Cheyutha
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for senior citizens, widows, disabled, beedi workers, single women, and others
promised: ₹4,000/month pension to senior citizens, widows, disabled, beedi workers, single women, weavers, AIDS/Filaria patients, dialysis patients
delivered: Pension amounts increased from BRS-era ₹2,016 but conflicting reports on whether full ₹4,000 achieved universally. A February 2026 report describes the hike as 'slated to increase' in the upcoming budget — suggesting the full amount has not been reached. Coverage expanded but many eligible beneficiaries still excluded.

name: Free Bus Travel for Women
description: Free travel for all women on TSRTC buses statewide
promised: Free bus travel for all women on TSRTC services
delivered: Launched December 9, 2023 (day 2 in office). Serves ~35 lakh women daily. 222.50 crore cumulative free journeys. ₹7,422 crore reimbursed to TSRTC. The government's most visible and successful scheme.

name: Rajiv Arogyasri Expansion
description: Expand Arogyasri health insurance coverage from ₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh
promised: Expand Rajiv Arogyasri coverage to ₹10 lakh per family
delivered: Coverage expanded to ₹10 lakh (from ₹5 lakh). 1,835 treatments covered across 1,410 empaneled hospitals. Benefiting ~90 lakh families.

name: Caste Census
description: Conduct comprehensive caste-based socio-economic survey
promised: Comprehensive caste census of all households in Telangana
delivered: Completed November-December 2024. Covered 3.54 crore individuals across 1.12 crore households. Telangana became the first state to publish comprehensive caste data.

name: ₹500 Gas Cylinder
description: LPG gas cylinders at ₹500 for eligible families
promised: LPG gas cylinders at ₹500 for all eligible households
delivered: Launched February 27, 2024. Targets ~40 lakh families with white ration cards.

name: New Ration Cards
description: Issue new ration cards to eligible families excluded from PDS
promised: Ration cards for all excluded eligible families
delivered: ~8 lakh new ration cards issued since January 2025.

name: Crop Loan Waiver
description: Complete waiver of crop loans up to ₹2 lakh within 100 days
promised: Waive crop loans up to ₹2 lakh for all farmer families within 100 days of taking office
delivered: Disbursed ~₹20,617 crore to ~25 lakh farmers in four phases through November 2024. Target: ₹31,000 crore for ~50 lakh farmers — roughly 25 lakh farmers still waiting. Food security card requirement excluded many eligible farmers. Timeline: 100 days missed; took 11+ months.

name: 2 Lakh Government Jobs
description: Fill 2,00,000 government posts in the first year
promised: 2,00,000 government jobs to be filled in Year 1 (by December 2024)
delivered: ~57,946 posts filled by March 2025 (29%). Group 1: 562 posts. Group 4: 8,084. Mega DSC: 10,006 teachers. SC sub-categorization froze new recruitment Oct 2024-Apr 2025. Police (12,452 vacancies), Group 2, additional DSC, and forest beat officer recruitments remain pending.

name: ₹4,000 Unemployment Allowance
description: Monthly unemployment allowance of ₹4,000 to educated unemployed youth
promised: ₹4,000/month unemployment allowance to educated unemployed youth
delivered: Zero implementation in 818+ days. Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka publicly denied in December 2024 that Congress had made this promise, contradicting recorded statements by Priyanka Gandhi during the election campaign. No application process, no budget allocation, no pilot programme.

keyMetrics.label: Government Response
keyMetrics.actual: Deputy CM denied promise was made

name: Vidya Bharosa Cards
description: Education cards worth ₹5 lakh for students to cover educational expenses
promised: Vidya Bharosa cards worth ₹5 lakh per student for educational expenses
delivered: Zero implementation in 818+ days. No application process established. No pilot programme. No budget allocation. No distribution.

name: Old Pension Scheme Restoration
description: Restore the Old Pension Scheme (OPS) for government employees
promised: Restore Old Pension Scheme for ~2.2 lakh CPS (Contributory Pension Scheme) employees
delivered: Zero implementation in 818+ days. No Government Order issued. No committee formed. No timeline provided. Affects ~2.2 lakh government employees currently under CPS.

name: International Schools in Every Mandal
description: Establish Telangana International Schools in all 612 mandals
promised: International-standard schools in all 612 mandals
delivered: Promise quietly replaced by 'Young India Integrated Residential Schools' (YIIRS) — one per Assembly constituency (119 total, not 612). Foundation stones laid for 28 schools in October 2024. None operational as of March 2026.

name: Teacher Recruitment (Mega DSC)
description: Recruit 20,000 teachers through Mega DSC to fill school vacancies
promised: 20,000 teacher posts through Mega DSC
delivered: 11,062 posts notified (Feb 2024). ~10,006 teachers received appointment letters by October 2024. 19,000+ teacher posts remain vacant. Second DSC notification (scheduled Feb 2025) not issued as of March 2026.

name: ₹2,500 Monthly Cash Transfer to Women
description: Monthly cash transfer of ₹2,500 to every woman head of household
promised: ₹2,500/month direct cash transfer to ~90 lakh women heads of household
delivered: Zero disbursements in 818+ days. Application forms were collected in January 2024, but no processing, no pilot, and no implementation steps taken. Estimated annual cost ~₹50,000 crore makes this promise financially unsustainable at current revenue levels.

name: Free Internet for Students
description: Free internet connectivity for all students in Telangana
promised: Free internet for all students across Telangana
delivered: Zero implementation. No scheme launched, no pilot programme, no budget allocation.

name: ₹12,000/Year for Agricultural Labour
description: Annual financial support of ₹12,000 to agricultural labour families
promised: ₹12,000/year to every agricultural labour family
delivered: Zero implementation. Part of the Rythu Bharosa guarantee but not addressed in the scheme's launch. No separate mechanism created for agricultural labourers.

name: Interest-Free Crop Loans up to ₹3 Lakh
description: Interest-free crop loans up to ₹3 lakh for farmers
promised: Interest-free crop loans up to ₹3 lakh for all farmers
delivered: Government has been providing interest subvention on crop loans but the full ₹3 lakh threshold with zero interest has not been universally implemented. Coverage remains patchy across districts.

name: Bonus Over MSP for Crops
description: Additional bonus above the central MSP for key crops
promised: Bonus over central MSP for paddy, cotton, and other key crops
delivered: No state-level bonus announced. Central MSP applies. BRS had provided a ₹500-₹1,000/quintal bonus for fine rice varieties.

name: SC/ST Sub-Plan Full Implementation
description: Full allocation and spending of SC/ST sub-plan funds proportional to population
promised: Full allocation and spending of SC/ST sub-plan funds per population proportion
delivered: Allocations increased in Budget 2024-25 but utilization remains below allocation. Dalit Bandhu scheme coverage expanded but at reduced amounts.

name: Complete BRS-Era Double Bedroom Houses
description: Complete the unfinished double bedroom housing projects started under BRS
promised: Complete all pending 2BHK double bedroom houses started by BRS government
delivered: Of ~2.8 lakh incomplete 2BHK units inherited, a small fraction have been completed. Many projects remain stalled due to contractor disputes and land issues.

name: Dharani Portal Reform
description: Reform the Dharani land records portal to address grievances and enable transparent land transactions
promised: Overhaul Dharani portal to fix land record errors, enable sub-registrar involvement, and process pending mutation requests
delivered: Committee formed under Akunuri Murali to study reforms. Some fixes to mutation errors implemented. Sub-registrar offices restored for certain functions. But fundamental portal issues remain — farmers report ongoing inability to correct land records.

name: ₹1/kg Rice (continued)
description: Continue providing rice at ₹1 per kg under PDS
promised: Continue ₹1/kg rice through PDS for all ration card holders
delivered: Scheme continued. ~87 lakh ration card families continue to receive rice at ₹1/kg through the PDS network.

name: Fee Reimbursement for Students
description: Continue and expand fee reimbursement scheme for students from economically weaker sections
promised: Fee reimbursement for all eligible students; clear pending arrears from BRS era
delivered: Scheme continued but pending arrears of ~₹5,000 crore from BRS era not fully cleared. Current disbursements ongoing but with delays. Private college operators report payment backlogs.

name: Police Recruitment (12,452 Posts)
description: Fill 12,452 vacant police constable and SI posts
promised: Recruit 12,452 police constables and sub-inspectors to fill vacancies
delivered: Notification not issued as of March 2026. SC sub-categorization court case delayed the process. Posts remain vacant.

name: Group 2 Recruitment
description: Complete Group 2 recruitment for government positions
promised: Conduct Group 2 recruitment exams and fill posts
delivered: Group 2 exam notification not issued. TSPSC reform committee was formed but recruitment process has not commenced.

name: Fitness Centres in Every Constituency
description: Establish Fit India centres with gym and sports facilities in every Assembly constituency
promised: Fitness centres in all 119 Assembly constituencies
delivered: Zero implementation. No centres established or announced.

name: Minority Welfare Enhancement
description: Increase scholarships and establish minority residential schools
promised: Enhanced minority scholarships, new minority residential schools, Shadi Mubarak enhanced to ₹1 lakh
delivered: Shadi Mubarak enhanced from ₹1 lakh to ₹1.5 lakh for minority girls. Some scholarship increases announced. New minority residential schools: announced but none operational.

name: Musi Riverfront Development
description: Clean and develop Musi River with a world-class riverfront
promised: Clean Musi River and develop a Thames-like riverfront in Hyderabad
delivered: Musi Riverfront Development Corporation formed. Estimated cost: ₹1.5 lakh crore. Massive demolition drives along the river displaced thousands of families. Environmental clearances pending. Actual river cleaning: minimal progress. Project has become a major controversy.

name: Outer Ring Road Phase 2
description: Build Phase 2 of Hyderabad Outer Ring Road
promised: Complete ORR Phase 2 connecting remaining areas around Hyderabad
delivered: DPR (Detailed Project Report) under preparation. No construction started. Land acquisition not initiated.

name: PHC/CHC Upgradation
description: Upgrade Primary and Community Health Centres across all districts
promised: Upgrade PHCs and CHCs to provide 24/7 services with adequate staff and equipment
delivered: Some upgrades initiated in select districts. Staff vacancies remain high — ~40% of specialist doctor posts vacant in rural CHCs. Equipment procurement in progress for ~200 centres.

name: Hyderabad Metro Expansion
description: Extend Hyderabad Metro to Old City and Airport
promised: Extend metro to Old City (Falaknuma), Airport, and other areas
delivered: DPR approved for Airport Express line (~31 km). Old City extension: DPR stage. No construction started on any new corridor. Airport line estimated at ₹6,250 crore.

name: White Paper on Kaleshwaram
description: Release white paper on Kaleshwaram project irregularities and fix structural issues
promised: Publish white paper exposing Kaleshwaram cost overruns and structural failures
delivered: Justice Ghose Commission report submitted but not made fully public. Partial findings released showing cost escalation from ₹38,000 cr to ₹1.47 lakh cr. Medigadda barrage repairs ongoing. No comprehensive white paper published.
